name: Cat-C orthogonal-real fixes
operator_decision: Option A applied 2026-04-29 — both M4 escalations relabeled as Cat-B carry-forwards; M4/M5 stop-threshold raised from 33 to 35.
rationale: M4 closed all FIX-disposition Cat-C entries from M1-orthogonal-real-failures.json EXCEPT 2 entries that root-caused to known coverage gaps in prior milestones (vuln-migration-v1 + FAI-v1). Per dispatch-contract validator mandate operator_escalation_on_regression_from_prior_milestone, those 2 entries were ESCALATED rather than silently fixed. Operator decision (Option A): relabel both as Cat-B carry-forwards owned by their respective sibling/follow-on milestones (vuln-source-parity-v1 absorbs the Python f-string-return XSS gap; ruby-bare-call-extraction-v1 TBD addresses the documented FAI-v1 M5 bare-`gets` carry-forward). M4 closes successfully with cargo test failure-count = 35 EXACTLY (the new Cat-B baseline).

named_exception_files_modified.change_summary: git_log + git_log_numstat treat 'does not have any commits yet' stderr (empty repo) as Ok(empty) per dispatch-contract M4 named exception list.
analyze_dead_code returns Ok(empty Report) on empty input/no-language detection.
compute_martin_metrics returns Ok(empty Report) on empty input/no-language detection.
parse_coverage accepts directory paths (auto-discovers known coverage filenames); returns Ok(empty) when no coverage file found.

Removed else_clause from cognitive complexity base_increment match per SonarSource Cognitive Complexity v1.4 spec; added is_else_if check so `else if` (if_statement under else_clause) keeps +1 base with no nesting penalty.

delete_on_stale: test_kotlin_returns_unsupported (Kotlin now supported via tree_sitter_kotlin_ng; replacement test_kotlin_parse exists)
test_swift_returns_unsupported (Swift now supported via tree_sitter_swift; replacement test_swift_parse exists)
test_search_with_extension_filter (legacy --ext flag; SmartSearch has no extension filter)
Note: 5 above are inline #[cfg(test)] mod tests inside cli_search_context_tests.rs replaced by a documenting comment block.

escalated_to_operator.operator_decision: Option A — relabel both as Cat-B carry-forwards; raise stop-threshold from 33 to 35; defer fixes to follow-on milestones (vuln-source-parity-v1 absorbs Python f-string-return XSS gap; ruby-bare-call-extraction-v1 TBD addresses bare-`gets` AST limitation per FAI-v1 M5 carry-forward).

cargo_test_workspace_features_semantic_n.summary: 35 failed EXACTLY (post-amendment Cat-B baseline: 33 vuln-source-parity-v1 originals + test_vuln_detects_xss + ruby_io_popen_with_user_input_via_compute_taint). All ~1900 other tests pass.

validator_mandate_compliance.no_test_gaming_rule: PASS — zero #[ignore] markers added; assertions corrected to match post-fix correct behavior or the SonarSource Cognitive Complexity v1.4 spec; no commented-out code; no weakened assertions.
validator_mandate_compliance.no_source_changes_in_active_subcmd_imple: PASS — source modifications restricted to (a) named exceptions: cognitive analyzer, parse_coverage, analyze_dead_code, compute_martin_metrics, git_log; (b) test fixtures (test files, #[cfg(test)] mods, fixture data files). Two regressions that would have required active-subcmd-handler changes were ESCALATED instead of silently fixed.
validator_mandate_compliance.pre_publish_baseline_restored: PASS — failure-count is 35; stop-threshold raised 33->35 per operator Option A. The 2 added Cat-B entries are documented carry-forwards owned by sibling/follow-on milestones.
validator_mandate_compliance.cli_output_shape_unchanged: PASS — no CLI public-API surface changes; all source changes are internal handler edge-cases (named exceptions only).
validator_mandate_compliance.operator_escalation_on_regression_from_p: RESOLVED — reports/operator-escalation-regression.json triggered, diagnosed, and closed via Option A (relabel as Cat-B carry-forwards).

next_steps: M5 (CHANGELOG + local tag workspace-test-infrastructure-v1) proceeds with the new Cat-B baseline of 35 failures.
Sibling milestone vuln-source-parity-v1 absorbs test_vuln_detects_xss (Python f-string-return XSS sink coverage gap — 1 lang scope expansion).
Follow-on milestone ruby-bare-call-extraction-v1 (TBD) addresses bare-`gets` AST limitation per documented FAI-v1 M5 carry-forward.
